advantages: Conflicts force a human decision when automated merging is unsafe — they prevent silent data loss.
The three-way merge algorithm resolves most overlaps automatically; conflicts are exceptional, not normal.
Tooling has matured: `diff3` markers, mergetool, `nbdime`, `rerere` all reduce friction.
Resolving conflicts deepens your understanding of *intent* on both sides — sometimes catches real bugs.
disadvantages: Long-lived branches accumulate conflict pain non-linearly.
Rebases re-surface conflicts per commit — exhausting on big branches.
Auto-merging without reading the diff can silently keep the wrong side.
Binary and notebook conflicts are awful without specialized tooling.
